assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Your entire multislice job will be terminated (all 4 slices simultaneously)' and 'TPU VMs will be drained and rescheduled to different physical hardware', indicating atomic recreation rather than rolling upgrade

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Your entire multislice job will be terminated (all 4 slices simultaneously)', indicating that maintenance affects all slices together

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: Response states 'Your entire multislice job will be terminated (all 4 slices simultaneously)' and 'The job won't automatically resume', clearly indicating simultaneous restart and interruption

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: Response does not mention resource availability issues or potential blocking/extended downtime scenarios during recreation

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response recommends 'Implement Robust Checkpointing' and discusses checkpoint frequency, though it doesn't explicitly say 'immediately before maintenance window'

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Response includes recovery planning section with scripts to 'Load from latest checkpoint' and 'Ensure your training script detects and loads latest checkpoint'

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: Response does not mention maintenance exclusions or the option to defer maintenance events

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Maintenance windows are usually scheduled during low-usage periods' and includes a section on 'Consider Maintenance Windows' with configuration examples
